title: From Observing Dominion to Fulfilling Creator's Mandate

current_reality.faith: Christian, Jewish, or Muslim - Abrahamic tradition
current_reality.daily_life: Pray, worship, work secular job, try to live faith, struggle with modern world
current_reality.religious_practice: Regular worship, try to follow teachings, feel inadequate
current_reality.soul_conflict: Commanded to be stewards, watching creation destroyed - feel helpless
current_reality.current_work: Secular job that doesn't align with faith values
current_reality.future: Keep trying to live faith in world hostile to it, die knowing you didn't fulfill calling

theological_tension: Genesis 1:26-30 - 'Have dominion', 'In image of Creator' - but what have we CREATED?
The Creator CREATES - we're in His image - but our creation is factory farms, pollution, slaughter
Commanded to care for creation - watching it destroyed daily
Islamic concept of Khalifa (stewardship) - humans as Allah's representatives - but look at what we've done
Jewish Tikkun Olam (repair the world) - commanded to fix it, but overwhelmed by scale
Called to be co-creators with God - currently destroying instead of creating
the_two_questions: Question 1: 'Is THIS our work of creation?' - factory farms, slaughter, pollution, extinction
Answer: NO - this is perversion of creative mandate
Question 2: 'What SHOULD be the Dream of Work of Creation?' - what world would honor Creator?
Answer: Paradise - all life fed, no killing, deserts blooming, peaceable kingdom
immediate: Work secular job - doesn't serve God, serves mammon (profit/extraction)
Watch creation destroyed - helpless to stop it individually
Eat meat - complicit in killing creatures God made
Modern life - constant compromise of values
Isolated in faith - secular world dominant, faith marginalized
deeper: Called to stewardship - failing at it
Made in Creator's image - but creating hell not heaven
Faith community fragmented - can't coordinate for large action
Guilt - know we should do better, don't know how
existential: Face God having failed stewardship - 'what did you do with My creation?'
Die knowing you were called to build paradise, built nothing
Children inherit destroyed creation - you failed to repair for next generation
Faith lived individually - never manifested collectively at scale
Legacy: Believed in God, failed to do His work

Creator God - made in His image to CREATE - BARRACKS is creating paradise
Khalifa (Islamic) - stewardship - BARRACKS is responsible stewardship
Tikkun Olam (Jewish) - repair the world - BARRACKS repairs at planetary scale
Peaceable Kingdom (Isaiah 11) - lion lying with lamb - BARRACKS makes this REAL
This is literally fulfilling your faith's commands

Genesis 2:15 - 'Work and take care of Eden' - we're BUILDING Eden
Isaiah 11:6-9 - Peaceable kingdom - we're ESTABLISHING it via food abundance
Romans 8:19-22 - Creation groaning, awaiting redemption - we're DOING the redemption
Quran 6:38 - All creatures are communities like you - we're FEEDING those communities
This is NOT secular - this is SACRED WORK

theological: Death is the enemy (1 Corinthians 15:26) - we're fighting it
Defend the defenseless - animals, unborn, we're doing it
Repair creation - Tikkun Olam, we're doing it
Steward responsibly - Khalifa, we're doing it
